user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You're going away for a few weeks and want to PAUSE your meal-kit subscription for now -- you are NOT canceling it, you just want deliveries to stop temporarily and to be able to resume later. You do NOT want to change anything else about your account.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the problem in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- can you pause my subscription while I'm traveling?"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal subscription ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your subscription and find it.
- Make it clear you want to PAUSE, not cancel. If the agent seems about to cancel instead, correct them -- you want to be able to come back.
- Let the agent do the work of looking up your account and your subscription. Don't hand it everything in the first message.
- If the agent offers to change anything else, politely decline -- just pause the subscription.
- Once the agent confirms your subscription is paused, you're satisfied and done.
